seoTitle: How to Insert a File in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Embed live document preview cards in Microsoft Loop using slash commands or drag-and-drop uploads, backed by OneDrive and SharePoint sync.
intro: Keeping reference files buried in disconnected folders makes it harder for your team to find what they need. This guide covers inserting a file into a Loop page as a live preview card, either through the slash command picker or by dragging a file straight from your desktop.

steps.heading: Navigate to your document section
steps.body: Open your target Loop page canvas and go to the specific section where you want to add the file.

steps.heading: Use the slash command
steps.body: Type /file and select the Upload or Link option from the component picker to start the sync.

steps.heading: Or drag and drop
steps.body: Drag a file straight from your desktop folder into the active Loop browser canvas to auto-upload it to OneDrive.

steps.heading: Insert the preview card
steps.body: Select your file from the recent document popup and click Insert to render the live embedded preview card on your page.

steps.heading: Adjust access permissions
steps.body: Click the three dots icon on the preview card to adjust who can view or edit the attached file, or rename its display label.

faq.question: Do files inserted this way stay live-linked to the original?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop bridges OneDrive for Business and SharePoint so inserted files render live previews rather than static copies.

faq.question: Can I insert a file just as a hyperlink instead of a preview card?
faq.answer: Yes, you can toggle the display mode between a visual preview card, a hyperlink text anchor, or a downloadable asset badge.

faq.question: What file types can I insert this way?
faq.answer: The feature supports common cloud-stored documents, including Word, Excel, and PDF files, among others.

faq.question: Why can't a teammate access a file I inserted?
faq.answer: This is usually a permissions issue on the underlying file, so check the sharing settings inside the preview card's options menu.
